statement.en: EFFECT OF TRADITIONAL VERSUS ELECTRONIC COGNITIVE AIDS ON OBJECTIVE MEASURES OF BASIC AND ADVANCED LIFE SUPPORT IN A HIGH-FIDELITY SIMULATION OF CANINE CARDIOPULMONARY ARREST
statement.summary: Objective: To evaluate differences in cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) performance and user comfort with the use of traditional (TCA) versus electronic cognitive aids (ECA) in a simulation of canine cardiopulmonary arrest in Reassessment Campaign on Veterinary Resuscitation (RECOVER) certified novice veterinary rescuers (CRs).

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a preprint empirical study (dated 2026, future date indicates provisional/speculative status) presenting original experimental results on cognitive aid effectiveness in veterinary CPR training. The claim is not yet peer-reviewed or validated in published literature. The comparative outcome (TCA vs ECA performance) constitutes a factual claim about observable phenomena, but lacks established consensus and formal publication status required for TRUE classification. The study design is sound but results remain provisional.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 紙と電子の間で、身体の記憶が視線と競い合う。
